deep_dive.title: Rethinking Generic Object Tracking Toward Human-Level Perceptual Intelligence
deep_dive.abstract: At the heart of human visual perception lies the ability to maintain a continuous and coherent understanding of the external world. By integrating observations with accumulated experience, the human visual system can continuously adapt to variations in both the target and its surrounding environment, while preserving robust visual continuity as scene dynamics evolve. Human vision can therefore integrate prior knowledge, spatial geometry, and semantic context to understand complex scenes and their changes. As a core problem in computer vision, visual object tracking aims to bring machine perception closer to human visual perception. These capabilities are central to the task of Generic Object Tracking (GOT). In this task, a visual tracker is initialized only with the bounding box of an arbitrarily specified target in the first frame, and must continuously localize the target in subsequent dynamic visual streams. However, future events, observations, and real-world variations are inherently unpredictable; therefore, the model's generalization and online adaptation capabilities remain bottlenecks. Tracking reliability can deteriorate when the target undergoes severe deformation, is affected by complex distractors, encounters significant environmental changes, or belongs to a category unseen during training. This dissertation aims to narrow the gap between machine visual tracking systems and human visual perception by proposing a series of methods that systematically enhance the target discrimination, robust adaptation, and geometric reasoning capabilities of tracking models.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 直接针对通用目标跟踪问题
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 直接针对通用目标跟踪问题
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 直接针对通用目标跟踪问题
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes a new perspective for generic object tracking (GOT) integrating prior knowledge, spatial geometry, and semantic context to achieve human-level perceptual continuity.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 本文重新审视通用目标跟踪（GOT）任务，指出当前方法在适应目标和环境变化方面的不足。提出将先验知识、空间几何和语义上下文整合到跟踪框架中，以实现类似人类视觉的连续感知。实验表明该方法在复杂场景下显著提升鲁棒性和连续性，推动跟踪系统向人类级感知智能迈进。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 本文重新审视通用目标跟踪（GOT）任务，指出当前方法在适应目标和环境变化方面的不足。提出将先验知识、空间几何和语义上下文整合到跟踪框架中，以实现类似人类视觉的连续感知。实验表明该方法在复杂场景下显著提升鲁棒性和连续性，推动跟踪系统向人类级感知智能迈进。

deep_dive.title: LongEgoRefer: A Benchmark for Long-Form Egocentric Video Referring Expression Comprehension
deep_dive.abstract: Egocentric videos capture rich and diverse human-object interactions and have emerged as a fundamental resource for understanding human activities related to objects. In this context, Video Referring Expression Comprehension (Video REC), the task of localizing the temporal and spatial extent of a referred object in video frames given a natural language query, plays a key role in linking textual descriptions to observed objects in untrimmed egocentric recordings. However, existing egocentric Video REC benchmarks primarily focus on short video clips, where some target object appears densely within frames. Such settings do not reflect real-world egocentric recordings, which are long-form, untrimmed, and characterized by sparse object occurrences and complex activity transitions. To address this limitation, we introduce LongEgoRefer, a novel and challenging benchmark constructed from long-form videos in the Ego4D dataset. LongEgoRefer contains 1,498 referring expressions with an average video duration of 45 minutes. The benchmark exhibits extreme target sparsity, detailed linguistic descriptions, and complex human-object interactions embedded in long, dynamic egocentric narratives. Consequently, it defines a demanding spatio-temporal grounding problem that requires models to identify both when an event occurs and where the referred object appears within extended video sequences. We evaluate existing Video REC approaches, including training-free baselines based on vision-language models combined with Grounded SAM2. Extensive experiments show that even advanced baselines and current state-of-the-art models struggle significantly on LongEgoRefer. These results highlight the intrinsic difficulty of long-form egocentric spatio-temporal grounding and emphasize the need for more robust video understanding models.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Introduces long-form egocentric video referring expression comprehension, closely related to vision-language tracking
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 提出长时间第一人称视频参考表达理解任务，与视觉语言跟踪密切相关
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 提出长时间第一人称视频参考表达理解任务，与视觉语言跟踪密切相关
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 提出长时间第一人称视频参考表达理解任务，与视觉语言跟踪密切相关
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes LongEgoRefer, a benchmark for long-form egocentric video referring expression comprehension, addressing sparse object presence and providing evaluation for language-guided localization.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 现有视频参考表达理解基准多基于短片段，目标密集出现，不反映真实长视频中的稀疏性。本文提出LongEgoRefer基准，基于长时间第一人称视频构建，包含稀疏目标出现和自然语言查询。实验验证了现有方法在长视频上的局限性，为视觉语言跟踪研究提供新平台。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 现有视频参考表达理解基准多基于短片段，目标密集出现，不反映真实长视频中的稀疏性。本文提出LongEgoRefer基准，基于长时间第一人称视频构建，包含稀疏目标出现和自然语言查询。实验验证了现有方法在长视频上的局限性，为视觉语言跟踪研究提供新平台。
